sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see a new area! At the bottom there's a purple strip with many "R" (dark red) or similar colored blocks. The player is now on the right side of the corridor.
Looking at the image:
- 3 purple blocks with yellow centers at top-left
- 2 rows of green N blocks in the middle
- Player on the right side of the lower corridor
- Purple strip at the bottom with many small blocks
The N blocks are still all there (14 total). Clicking them caused game over before. Let me reconsider the mechanics.
Maybe the goal is to navigate the player to the right side and interact with something there. Or maybe I need to use ACTION7 instead of MOUSE.
Let me check what's on the right side and try ACTION7.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after moving right
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Find player
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Player: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check what's at the bottom - the purple strip
# Count colors
color_counts = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    color_counts[n['color']] = color_counts.get(n['color'], 0) + n['pixels']
print(f"\nColor counts: {color_counts}")

# Try ACTION7 to see what it does
action(['ACTION7'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: ACTION7 is not a valid action despite being listed. Let me focus on what I can do: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE. The N blocks are still there. Let me try clicking one N block at a time, checking after each click if the game goes over.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click one N block at a time
seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks: {len(n_blocks)}")

# Click just one
if n_blocks:
    n = n_blocks[0]
    center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    print(f"Clicking N at ({center_r}, {center_c})")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c}])
    print(f"Result: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
N blocks: 14
Clicking N at (27, 15)
Result: game_over=False, board_changed=True
